category.name: LINQ Reporting
category.preferred_workflow: Create the template document programmatically inside the example unless an external template file is explicitly bootstrapped by the example. Create realistic local sample data and local sample assets before calling ReportingEngine.BuildReport. Keep root object, root name, public model classes, and template references fully aligned.
category.secondary_rules_purpose: This is a secondary delta rules file. It should be combined with the main linq-reporting rules only when a task fails completely under the primary rules. Preserve the existing safest primary rule: when using foreach for repeated tabular content, start foreach first, start table after foreach starts, end table just before foreach ends, then close foreach.

primary_core_rules: Use Aspose.Words.Reporting.ReportingEngine for all LINQ Reporting examples.
Always insert real LINQ Reporting tags into the document template before calling BuildReport.
Use only valid Aspose.Words LINQ Reporting syntax with <<...>> tags.
Close foreach blocks using <</foreach>> exactly; do not use </foreach> or any HTML/XML-style closing syntax.
Do not use mustache syntax like {{...}} for LINQ Reporting templates.
Do not call BuildReport until the template content has been fully created and saved or loaded.
primary_template_rules: If a template file is required by the scenario, create it programmatically inside the example first, then save it locally, then load it again for report generation if needed.
Do not assume any template file already exists on disk.
When the task mentions DOCX templates, create a valid DOCX template in the example using Document and DocumentBuilder.
When creating tags with DocumentBuilder.Writeln or Write, use the exact <<...>> syntax required by the LINQ Reporting Engine.
Do not introduce malformed tags, mismatched opening/closing tags, or invalid in-tag expressions.
primary_alignment_rules: Keep the BuildReport root object, root name, and template references fully aligned.
If the template uses <<[order.CustomerName]>>, call BuildReport(doc, order, "order").
If the template uses <<[model.CustomerName]>>, call BuildReport(doc, model, "model").
If a wrapper object is passed, template expressions must reference that wrapper explicitly instead of assuming the wrapped object is the root.
Avoid anonymous root data sources; use a public wrapper class when needed.
primary_tag_rules: Use valid tags such as <<foreach [p in persons]>>, <<if [condition]>>, <<var [x = expr]>>, <<[expr]>>, and matching closing tags like <</foreach>> and <</if>> when supported by the engine.
Do not place pseudo-C# code outside valid tag syntax and expect the engine to interpret it.
Do not write malformed tag examples such as {{foreach p in Model}}...{{end}}.
When nesting loops or conditions, ensure closing tags are properly balanced.
Use only valid in-tag expressions that the LINQ Reporting Engine can evaluate.
Do not use pseudo-format suffixes like <<[value]:date>> or <<[value]:currency>>.
Do not use composite format placeholders like <<[value]:{0:C}>> or <<[dateValue]:{0:yyyy-MM-dd}>>.
For formatted output, use expression-based formatting such as <<[value.ToString("C")]>> or <<[dateValue.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")]>>.
For sorting, filtering, projection, grouping, and aggregate scenarios, keep expressions simple and valid for the actual model shape.
primary_runtime_prevention: Do not assume templates already exist; generate them.
Do not leave model properties inaccessible to the reporting engine.
Do not mismatch BuildReport root name and template references.
Do not create malformed LINQ Reporting tags.
Do not forget to create sample input files referenced by insert, image, JSON, XML, or CSV scenarios.
secondary_delta_focus: Hard bans for recurring wrong XML and data-shape mistakes

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Parser errors caused by malformed tags, mismatched closing tags, extra colons/commas, invented switches, or pseudo-tag variants.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use only exact LINQ Reporting tag syntax and valid in-tag expressions. Keep opening and closing tags matched and avoid invented switches.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Errors such as EndTable while not building a table, or foreach crossing table/story boundaries.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use the safest repeated-table structure: start foreach first, start the table after foreach starts, end the table just before foreach ends, then close foreach. Keep each opening and closing tag within the same valid table/story structure.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Image tag not well formed, image-container errors, or malformed image-in-table structure.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Place image tags only inside a textbox prepared as the image container. For image-in-table tasks, create the table safely and place the textbox inside the required cell.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Template paths do not match the actual root object, root name, or source shape, especially for XML, DataTable, DataView, or DataRow sources.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Keep the BuildReport root object, root name, and template expressions fully aligned. Use public model types and match template paths to the real source hierarchy.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Compile or runtime failures caused by unsupported XML namespace APIs or incorrect XmlDataSource constructor usage.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Use supported XmlDataSource constructors together with XmlDataLoadOptions when needed. Do not pass XmlNamespaceManager to XmlDataSource constructors and do not call unavailable namespace helper APIs.

common_failure_patterns.symptom: Warnings such as CS8618, CS8600, CS8602, CS8604, or IDE0090.
common_failure_patterns.fix: Initialize non-nullable properties, guard maybe-null values before dereference, use nullable locals when appropriate, and prefer target-typed new where the simplified form is obvious.
